seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Agentic RAG with OpenAI Assistants API enables multi-step retrieval agents that autonomously plan queries, fetch from multiple sources, and synthesize attributed responses using built-in tools like file search and function calling. However, the black-box nature of assistant runs introduces risks like hallucinated citations, irrelevant retrievals, and unsafe code execution. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure source faithfulness, query accuracy, and safety before responses reach users.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on an OpenAI Assistant configured with file search for vector retrieval and custom functions for multi-source querying (e.g., web search, enterprise DBs). A persistent thread maintains conversation state as the agent iteratively plans retrieval queries, executes tools, and synthesizes responses with citations. Run completion triggers a Vex webhook for verification before final output.

Typical flow: User query → Assistant plans sub-queries → Parallel tool calls (file_search + functions) → Rerank + synthesize → Vex verifies faithfulness/consistency → Deliver response. This autonomy excels for complex Q&A but amplifies risks from unverified tool outputs and context bloat.

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant documents
risks.description: Agent generates broad queries pulling outdated or off-topic files, degrading response quality
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies semantic relevance between retrieved chunks and final answer

risks.name: Hallucinated function call arguments
risks.description: Assistant fabricates JSON args outside tool schemas, causing API errors or data leaks
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation + hallucination detection on tool outputs

risks.name: Thread context exceeding token limits
risks.description: Cumulative tool outputs bloat thread silently, causing incoherent responses
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and enforces context truncation policies

risks.name: Unverified source attribution
risks.description: Agent claims citations from non-retrieved sources, eroding trust
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-checks citations against actual file_search results

implementation.title: Create Agentic RAG Assistant
implementation.description: Configure assistant with file search and planning function for multi-step retrieval

implementation.title: Upload Knowledge Files
Run Thread with Webhook
implementation.description: Execute agent run and trigger Vex verification
implementation.code:
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id,
    webhook_override=vex_webhook_url  # Vex verification endpoint
)

# Poll for completion
client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id)

implementation.title: TypeScript Assistant Creation

vexIntegration.title: Install Vex SDK
vexIntegration.description: Setup verification client for OpenAI webhooks
vexIntegration.code:
pip install openai vex-sdk

from vex import Vex

vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

# Create verification policy
policy_id = vex.policies.create({
  "name": "Agentic RAG Guardrails",
  "verifications": [
    "source_faithfulness",
    "hallucination_detection",
    "citation_verification",
    "query_planning_accuracy"
  ],
  "risk_profile": "high"
})
